subject: ap-psychology
title: Research Methods & Experimental Design
stimulus:
Dr. Dawson is a psychologist who believes that students who read motivational statements before a test will
experience lower levels of anxiety as compared with students who read neutral statements. To examine this
belief, he conducted a study. In the study, he randomly assigned half of the participants to read motivational
statements and the other half to read neutral statements. Immediately after reading the statements, all of the
students completed a survey about their current level of anxiety (1 = low; 8 = high) and then took the test. Dr.
Dawson found statistically significant results that are depicted in the graph.

parts.prompt: State a valid operational definition of the dependent variable in this study.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Response must indicate that anxiety is defined by their score on the scale/survey of their anxiety.
Acceptable: The dependent variable can be operationally defined as the score on the anxiety scale.; The dependent variable is the score on the scale/survey.

parts.prompt: Identify the control group in this study.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Response must indicate that the control group is the group that reads the neutral statements.
Acceptable: The control group is the group that reads neutral statements.; The control group is the group that does not read positive statements.

parts.prompt: Explain why the study would be considered an experiment.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Response must indicate that the study is experimental because an independent variable is manipulated, and/or the participants were randomly assigned to the groups in the study.
Acceptable: The study is experimental because the students are randomly assigned to two; The study is an experiment because a variable was manipulated.

parts.prompt: Explain how the results of the study do or do not support Dr. Dawson’s hypothesis.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Response must indicate that Dr. Dawson’s hypothesis is supported because either the motivational statement group had lower anxiety or the neutral statement group had higher anxiety.
Acceptable: Dr. Dawson’s hypothesis is supported because the motivational group had lower; Dr. Dawson’s hypothesis is supported because the neutral statement group had higher

parts.prompt: The test Dr. Dawson gave was a vocabulary test. Explain how the following terms might relate to the students who performed poorly on the test in this study. Explain how External locus of control relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Response must indicate that students may perform poorly because they believe that factors outside of themselves (e.g., fate, luck, destiny, circumstances, other people) are responsible for their performance.
Acceptable: Students who believe that fate determines their scores on the test won’t perform as; Students who believe that the unfairness of their teacher determines their score won’t

parts.prompt: The test Dr. Dawson gave was a vocabulary test. Explain how the following terms might relate to the students who performed poorly on the test in this study. Explain how Stage one of Selye’s general adaptation syndrome relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Response must indicate that the students’ sympathetic nervous system or some specific aspect of their sympathetic nervous system is activated which interferes with taking the test. OR Response indicates that the students’ fight-or-flight mechanism is activated, which interferes with them taking the test. point.
Acceptable: Students’ fight or flight response might engage if they perceive the test as being a; Students’ heart rates increased, and they performed poorly.
parts.scoring_notes: NOTE: Students are not required to identify the name of the stage in order to earn the

parts.prompt: The test Dr. Dawson gave was a vocabulary test. Explain how the following terms might relate to the students who performed poorly on the test in this study. Explain how Crystallized intelligence relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Response must indicate that a lack of crystallized intelligence (e.g., accumulated knowledge, facts, general information, or vocabulary) could have hindered student performance on the test.
Acceptable: Students who study the wrong vocabulary perform poorly.; Students who do not have prior knowledge do poorly on the test.
